# Cycle 95 Brief

Cycle95 is complete and audited as:

```text
BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL
```

Banked lemma:

```text
L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-T1-EVAL-LIST
```

For `E=X-alpha`, `B=1`, and arbitrary anchor `w`, the support-wise
noncontained bad slopes are exactly:

```text
{ P(alpha) : deg P <= k, |{x in D : P(x)=w(x)}| >= k+sigma }.
```

Noncontainment is automatic. Thus the `t=1` anchored MCA wall collapses onto
the arbitrary-word locator/list wall at dimension `k+1`.

Current target:

```text
L-CYCLE96-ARBITRARY-WORD-NORM-SIEVE
```

Cycle96 should prove, kill, or sharply reduce the arbitrary-word replacement
for the monomial Galois/norm sieve. The missing point is archimedean control
for arbitrary word lifts.

1	# Cycle 95 Anchored Per-Fiber T=1 Audit
2	
3	## Verdict
4	
5	**BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL.**
6	
7	Cycle95 does not prove the arbitrary-anchor `t=1` polynomial bad-slope bound.
8	It gives a sharp exact reduction: the `t=1` anchored MCA cloud is precisely the
9	evaluation-at-`alpha` image of the arbitrary-word RS list at dimension `k+1`.
10	
11	Thus the `t=1` MCA wall is not a new independent wall. It collapses onto the
12	arbitrary-word locator/list wall.
13	
14	## Raw Artifacts
15	
16	Raw Fable artifacts were preserved under:
17	
18	```text
19	experimental/notes/m1/cycle95_anchored_perfiber_t1_raw/
20	```
21	
22	Run metadata:
23	
24	```text
25	run path: /Users/danielcabezas/packy-fable-ui/projects/rs-mca-proximity-prize-research/runs/2026-06-21T11-07-59-605Z-cycle95-anchored-perfiber-t1-c34e9889
26	model: claude-opus-4-8
27	mode: artifact_stream
28	status: OK
29	elapsed: 742196 ms
30	cost: 3.7723535 USD
31	capture warning: none
32	stop reason: end_turn
33	```
34	
35	Checksums were generated in:
36	
37	```text
38	experimental/notes/m1/cycle95_anchored_perfiber_t1_raw/SHA256SUMS.txt
39	```
40	
41	## Bankable Lemma
42	
43	```text
44	L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-T1-EVAL-LIST
45	```
46	
47	Let:
48	
49	```text
50	E = X - alpha
51	B = 1
52	alpha notin D
53	w: D -> F arbitrary
54	```
55	
56	Let `L_w` be the RS list of degree-`<=k` polynomials agreeing with `w` on at
57	least `k+sigma` points, equivalently the dimension-`k+1` arbitrary-word list:
58	
59	```text
60	L_w = { P in F[X] : deg P <= k, |{x in D : P(x)=w(x)}| >= k+sigma }.
61	```
62	
63	Then the support-wise noncontained bad slope set of:
64	
65	```text
66	y_z(x) = w(x)/(x-alpha) - z/(x-alpha)
67	```
68	
69	is exactly:
70	
71	```text
72	Z = { P(alpha) : P in L_w }.
73	```
74	
75	Consequently:
76	
77	```text
78	Lambda^NC_{1,delta}((X-alpha,1,w)) = |eval_alpha(L_w)| <= |L_w|.
79	```
80	
81	For nonzero constant `B`, slopes are only rescaled:
82	
83	```text
84	z = P(alpha)/B.

# Cycle 95 Anchored Per-Fiber T=1 Audit

## Verdict

**BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL.**

Cycle95 does not prove the arbitrary-anchor `t=1` polynomial bad-slope bound.
It gives a sharp exact reduction: the `t=1` anchored MCA cloud is precisely the
evaluation-at-`alpha` image of the arbitrary-word RS list at dimension `k+1`.

Thus the `t=1` MCA wall is not a new independent wall. It collapses onto the
arbitrary-word locator/list wall.

## Raw Artifacts

Raw Fable artifacts were preserved under:

```text
experimental/notes/m1/cycle95_anchored_perfiber_t1_raw/
```

Run metadata:

```text
run path: /Users/danielcabezas/packy-fable-ui/projects/rs-mca-proximity-prize-research/runs/2026-06-21T11-07-59-605Z-cycle95-anchored-perfiber-t1-c34e9889
model: claude-opus-4-8
mode: artifact_stream
status: OK
elapsed: 742196 ms
cost: 3.7723535 USD
capture warning: none
stop reason: end_turn
```

Checksums were generated in:

```text
experimental/notes/m1/cycle95_anchored_perfiber_t1_raw/SHA256SUMS.txt
```

## Bankable Lemma

```text
L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-T1-EVAL-LIST
```

Let:

```text
E = X - alpha
B = 1
alpha notin D
w: D -> F arbitrary
```

Let `L_w` be the RS list of degree-`<=k` polynomials agreeing with `w` on at
least `k+sigma` points, equivalently the dimension-`k+1` arbitrary-word list:

```text
L_w = { P in F[X] : deg P <= k, |{x in D : P(x)=w(x)}| >= k+sigma }.
```

Then the support-wise noncontained bad slope set of:

```text
y_z(x) = w(x)/(x-alpha) - z/(x-alpha)
```

is exactly:

```text
Z = { P(alpha) : P in L_w }.
```

Consequently:

```text
Lambda^NC_{1,delta}((X-alpha,1,w)) = |eval_alpha(L_w)| <= |L_w|.
```

For nonzero constant `B`, slopes are only rescaled:

```text
z = P(alpha)/B.
```

## Automatic Noncontainment

In this stratum, noncontainment is automatic on every support of size `>k`.
If `g=-1/(X-alpha)` were represented by a polynomial `G` of degree `<k` on
`S`, then:

```text
(X-alpha)G + 1
```

would vanish on more than `k` points while having degree at most `k`, forcing
an impossible identity.

## Route Cut

The `t=1` anchored wall is now identified with the arbitrary-word locator/list
wall. A proof of the `t=1` polynomial cloud bound requires the arbitrary-word
list theorem at dimension `k+1`, with quotient-periodic list cores charged.

This cuts the route:

```text
t=1 anchored MCA special argument
  -> full arbitrary-anchor residue-cloud upper theorem
```

unless that special argument actually proves the arbitrary-word list theorem.

## Codex Local Follow-Up

Codex added and ran:

```text
experimental/scripts/cycle95_t1_eval_list_check.py
```

The script enumerates small finite fields, arbitrary anchors `w`, and all
degree-`<=k` list codewords. It checks:

```text
bad slopes = {P(alpha): P in L_w}
```

and verifies automatic noncontainment on every support witness.

Run result:

```text
cases_checked: 70
total_bad_slopes_seen: 224
total_list_polys_seen: 235
PASS
```

The difference between list polynomials and bad slopes records exact
same-slope collisions from `P(alpha)=P'(alpha)`.

## Claim Level

Bank as:

```text
official-upper-side exact reduction
not a proof of t=1 RCUT
not a finite prize certificate
not a counterpacket
```

## Exact New Wall

Cycle95 points to:

```text
L-CYCLE96-ARBITRARY-WORD-NORM-SIEVE
```

Target statement:

Prove an arbitrary-word replacement for the monomial Galois/norm sieve. The
missing point is archimedean control for an arbitrary lift `U` of the word
`w`; the monomial proof uses small `{ -1,0,1 }` coefficients and has no direct
analogue once `w` is arbitrary.

Equivalently, prove the arbitrary-word locator/list theorem at dimension
`k+1`, after quotient cores are charged. A counterpacket would be an
aperiodic, non-quotient arbitrary word with a superpolynomial list whose
evaluation at `alpha` remains superpolynomial.

Let me read the start of Part II.Let me read the Part I rigidity and Galois-collision sieve sections, which contain the norm-sieve machinery that the Cycle94 plan says must be transferred.Let me read the Galois-amplified collision sieve (thm:no-collision and surrounding norm argument), which is the precise norm-sieve archimedean machinery the Cycle94 plan references for the monomial case.BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL
2	
3	I read `readme.md`/director state, `tex/slackMCA_v3.tex` (`def:mca`, `def:residue`, `thm:normalform`, `prop:noanchor`, `prob:perfiber`, `conj:B`, `conj:final-locator`/`conj:final-mca`, the slack calculus `thm:exactslack`/`lem:tlocator`/`thm:slackt`, the list side `def:locator-fiber`/`prop:arb-fiber`/`thm:qcore`/`cor:upstairs-poly`, and the Galois sieve `lem:galois-amp`/`thm:no-collision`/`prop:qfloor`), the Cycle94 audit and raw response, and the Cycle94 toy checker. Below is the Cycle95 result.
4	
5	## 1. Executive verdict and confidence
6	
7	I did not find an independent elementary proof of the `t=1` arbitrary‑anchor bound, and I can show cleanly why one should not exist by the proposed route. What I can bank is an **exact reduction**: the `t=1`, `E=X−α`, `B=1` residue cloud is, slope for slope, the evaluation‑at‑`α` image of the **dimension‑`(k+1)` arbitrary‑word locator list** of `w`. Three consequences are unconditional and checkable: (i) noncontainment is automatic in this stratum; (ii) the bad‑slope set is *exactly* `{P(α) : P∈List(RS[F,D,k+1], w) at agreement k+σ}`; (iii) hence the cloud has size `≤ |List|`. The headline finding is therefore that `L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-PERFIBER-COLLISION-T1` is **not a new wall** — it collapses onto the already‑isolated arbitrary‑word list local limit `conj:final-locator` (at dimension `k+1`), so the `t=1` MCA wall and the list wall are the same wall.
8	
9	Confidence: high on the reduction and on automatic noncontainment (elementary, and exactly what the Cycle94 toy checker already computes). High on the route cut (it is `prop:noanchor` made quantitative). Moderate‑to‑high that the only place a genuinely `t=1`‑specific theorem could beat `conj:final-locator` is the eval‑at‑`α` collapse, which I argue the adversary defeats by choosing `α` whenever `q` is large.
10	
11	## 2. Exact `t=1` theorem in source‑valid notation
12	
13	Fix the slope field `F=F_q` with `q=q_line=q_code=q_chal`, `C=RS[F,D,k]`, `D≤F^×` smooth generated‑field of order `n`, `k=ρn`, `s=k+σ`, `δ=1−(k+σ)/n=1−ρ−η`, `η=σ/n`. Take the datum `(E,B,w)=(X−α, 1, w)` with `α∈F∖D`, `w:D→F` arbitrary (`def:residue`, `t=1`). Write `L_w := List(RS[F,D,k+1], w, δ) = {P∈F[X] : deg P≤k, |{x∈D:P(x)=w(x)}|≥k+σ}` (the dimension‑`(k+1)` list of `w`; `def:locator-fiber`, `prop:arb-fiber` with `k→k+1`).
14	
15	L‑CYCLE95‑ANCHORED‑T1‑EVAL‑LIST (bankable, exact).
16	```
17	(a) Automatic noncontainment: for every witness with |S|>k, the direction word
18	    g = -B/E = -1/(X-α) is NOT explained by any G∈F_{<k}[X] on S; so every
19	    (k+σ)-agreement witness is noncontained.
20	(b) Exact slope identity: the set of support-wise MCA-bad slopes of the line
21	    y_z = w/(X-α) - z/(X-α) at radius δ is EXACTLY
22	        Z = { P(α) : P ∈ L_w }.
23	(c) Count: Λ^NC_{1,δ}((X-α,1,w)) = |Z| = |eval_α(L_w)| ≤ |L_w|.
24	(d) For a general nonzero constant B, z = P(α)/B; the count is identical
25	    (affine color rescaling).
26	```
27	
28	The exact source‑valid `t=1` RCUT statement is then conditional, and it is the cleanest sharp replacement:
29	```
30	THEOREM (t=1 anchored RCUT, conditional on conj:final-locator at dim k+1).
31	For aperiodic w (w not in a dimension-(k+1) quotient-core class, rem:aper),
32	σ≥Cn/log n and σ·log₂ q_gen ≥ (1+ε)·log₂ C(n,k+σ):
33	        Λ^NC_{1,1-ρ-η}((X-α,1,w)) ≤ |L_w| ≤ n^{B}.
34	The quotient-periodic w contribute the separated 2^{(β/H)Q_H(η)} term, exactly
35	as in conj:B / rem:aper, transferred through ρ→ρ+1/n.
36	```
37	
38	## 3. Proof of the bankable lemma, and the obstruction to the strong claim
39	
40	Proof of (a). A witness needs `G∈F_{<k}[X]` with `(x−α)G(x)=−1` for `x∈S`. Then `(X−α)G(X)+1` has degree `≤k` and vanishes on `S`, `|S|=k+σ>k`; a nonzero polynomial of degree `≤k` cannot have `>k` roots, so `(X−α)G+1≡0`, forcing `(X−α)∣1` — impossible. So `g=−1/(X−α)` is never contained on a `>k` support; noncontainment holds for free. (This is exactly why the simple‑pole direction is a *genuine* line, `def:mca`(ii) always active; it uses only `B≠0`, `deg B<t=1`, so it holds for every `t=1` datum.)
41	
42	Proof of (b),(c). A witness is `(Q_z,S_z)` with `deg Q_z<k+t=k+1`, `Q_z≡zB≡z (mod X−α)` i.e. `Q_z(α)=z`, and `Q_z=w` on `S_z`, `|S_z|≥k+σ`. So `Q_z` is a degree‑`≤k` polynomial agreeing with `w` on `≥k+σ` points: `Q_z∈L_w`, and `z=Q_z(α)`. Conversely any `P∈L_w` yields the bad slope `z=P(α)` with witness `(P, {P=w})` (noncontained by (a)). Distinct slopes force distinct `P` (since `z=P(α)`), so `Z=eval_α(L_w)` exactly, and `|Z|≤|L_w|`. ∎ This is precisely the computation in `cycle94_anchored_secant_toy_check.py`: for `t=1` it interpolates `w` on each support, keeps it iff `deg<k+1`, and reads the slope as `q_poly mod (X−α)=Q_S(α)`.
43	
44	Why the strong (unconditional) claim is blocked. By (b)–(c), bounding the aperiodic `t=1` cloud is bounding the **arbitrary‑word** dimension‑`(k+1)` list. Three facts pin this as genuinely the open list problem, not an easier secant problem:
45	
46	- We are *beyond Johnson*: radius `1−ρ−η` with `η=Θ(1/log n)` and `ρ+η<√ρ` for fixed `ρ`, so combinatorial list‑decoding bounds do not apply; quotient cores (`thm:qcore`) give superpolynomial lists here, exactly the periodic part we must charge.
47	- `prop:noanchor`: an arbitrary anchor `w` admits no word‑free/characteristic‑zero condition — `f=w/E` is an arbitrary word — so every prime‑averaging device (`thm:diag`, `prop:qfloor`, `thm:stable`, `thm:no-collision`) is structurally inapplicable. The monomial sieve `thm:no-collision` used coefficients in `{−1,0,1}` (`|N(G(ξ))|≤(2n)^{n/2M}`); the arbitrary‑word lift `U∈Z[ζ][X]` has *unbounded* archimedean coefficients, so the norm bound `‖h‖₁≤2s` of `prop:qfloor` has no analogue. This is the exact inhomogeneous obstruction the Cycle94 plan flagged, now localized to the missing archimedean bound.
48	- Cycle94 secant route: distinct slopes give codewords `P_i` with `|S_i∩S_j|≤k+t−1=k` (the difference vanishes on the common agreement and is degree `≤k`); the `S_i` are a constant‑weight code of distance `2σ`, which is GV‑exponential — so the secant bound alone yields nothing, and the extra fact "`P_i−P_j` does not vanish at `α`" does not improve the count. Route cut re‑confirmed at `t=1`.
49	
50	Can the anchor be removed / normalized / charged? Removed: no — `w=0` gives `Q_z≡0` and `z=0` only (`Λ=1`); the entire content is in `w`. Normalized: yes but harmlessly — adding a degree‑`≤k` codeword to `w` translates all slopes by `P_0(α)` (count‑invariant), and `B≠1` only rescales `z↦z/B`; so only the coset `w+RS[F,D,k+1]` (the syndrome) matters, still an arbitrary high‑degree word. Charged: the quotient‑periodic `w` (dimension‑`(k+1)` quotient cores) are separated into the `2^{(β/H)Q_H(η)}` term exactly as `rem:aper`/`conj:B`, leaving the aperiodic `n^{1+o(1)}` target.
51	
52	Is there a counterpacket? A source‑valid `t=1` counterpacket (aperiodic `w`, stable superpolynomial cloud, not quotient‑periodic) would be, by (b)–(c), an aperiodic superpolynomial dimension‑`(k+1)` list whose `eval_α` image stays superpolynomial — i.e. a refutation of `conj:final-locator`, the paper's expected positive theorem. The only known superpolynomial source is quotient cores, which are periodic and excluded. So no counterpacket is available here; producing one would be a far bigger event (killing the list conjecture), and I do not have a mechanism for it.
53	
54	## 4. Verification requirements
55	
56	- (a),(b),(c) are reproducible by the existing `cycle94_anchored_secant_toy_check.py` restricted to `t=1`: it already computes `slope = Q_S(α)` and groups supports by slope. Add two assertions: (i) for every random `(α,w)` over toy `F_p`, the set of bad slopes equals `{P(α)}` over the enumerated dimension‑`(k+1)` codeword list of `w` (re‑interpolate, dedupe by codeword), confirming the exact identity and `|Z|≤|codeword list|`; (ii) confirm noncontainment fires for *every* support (no `G` exists), i.e. the `t=1` cloud loses nothing to containment.
57	- The conditional theorem needs nothing new beyond `conj:final-locator`; to test the eval‑collapse question, extend `scripts/run_frontier.py` to sweep `α∈F∖D` for fixed superpolynomial quotient‑core `w` and record `|eval_α(list)|` vs `|list|` as `q` grows — this measures whether the projection can ever rescue an exponential list (it should not, for large `q`).
58	
59	## 5. Next exact lemma / construction
60	
61	The single statement that converts the `t=1` (and, with the dimension shift absorbed, the general) arbitrary‑anchor RCUT from PLAN to PROOF is the arbitrary‑word generalization of `thm:no-collision`:
62	
63	L‑CYCLE96‑ARBITRARY‑WORD‑NORM‑SIEVE (target).
64	```
65	Let p≡1 (mod n), H≅μ_n, U∈Z[ζ_n][X] a lift of an arbitrary word w:H→F_p,
66	deg U<n, dimension k+1. For (k+σ)-supports S,S' with deg(U mod L_S),
67	deg(U mod L_{S'})≤k and P_S≡P_{S'} (mod 𝔭): if σ≥Cn/log n and
68	p>exp(C·n log n/σ), then either S△S' is quotient-periodic (a union of cosets
69	of a subgroup of order M>σ), or P_S≡P_{S'} over Z[ζ].
70	```
71	The crux is exactly the missing archimedean control: replace the `{−1,0,1}`‑coefficient bound `‖h‖₁≤2s` (valid only for monomial‑prefix `U_c=X^s+…`) by a bound that survives an arbitrary lift `U`, or by a genuine fixed‑prime local‑limit substitute (the `rem:galois-limit` last `log n` factor). The smallest honest milestone toward it: prove the **eval‑collapse never helps** in the large‑field regime — i.e., for `q≥` a suitable polynomial threshold, `|eval_α(L_w)|=Θ(|L_w|)` for adversarial `α` — which shows the `t=1` cloud cannot be polynomially small unless `L_w` already is, closing the equivalence and proving that no `t=1`‑specific shortcut exists.
72	
73	Dependency DAG for Cycle95:
74	```
75	thm:normalform ── Λ^NC at one datum (E,B,w)
76	        │  t=1, E=X-α, B=1
77	        ▼
78	L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-T1-EVAL-LIST  (PROVED)
79	   bad slopes = eval_α(dimension-(k+1) arbitrary-word list of w)
80	   noncontainment automatic; secant/norm-sieve route CUT (=arbitrary-word list)
81	        │  collapses onto
82	        ▼
83	conj:final-locator (dim k+1)  ⟸  L-CYCLE96-ARBITRARY-WORD-NORM-SIEVE (open)
84	        │                         crux: archimedean control of arbitrary lift
85	        ▼
86	RCUT = W-OFFICIAL-MCA-MATCHING-UPPER-ARBITRARY-ANCHOR-RESIDUE-CLOUD
87	```
88	
89	## Self‑audit (six required questions)
90	
91	1. Proved: the exact identity `Λ^NC_{1,δ}((X−α,1,w)) = |eval_α(L_w)| ≤ |L_w|` and automatic noncontainment for every `t=1` datum (`B≠0`, `deg B<1`). Not proved: the `t=1` polynomial bound itself, in any nontrivial field regime — it is reduced to, and is morally as hard as, `conj:final-locator` at dimension `k+1`.
92	
93	2. Official‑prize‑relevant in direction (it is about `emca` over `thm:normalform`, `q_chal=q_line=q`, target `2^-128`), but the deliverable is a reduction lemma plus a route cut, not a finite counterpacket and not a positive threshold certificate. It is upper‑side progress that *identifies the wall*, not a finished safe‑side theorem.
94	
95	3. First line that could fail: none in L‑CYCLE95‑ANCHORED‑T1‑EVAL‑LIST (unconditional, degree count). In the conditional theorem the first failure point is `conj:final-locator` itself; concretely, the archimedean step of `prop:qfloor`/`thm:no-collision` (`‖h‖₁≤2s`) has no analogue for an arbitrary lift `U`, exactly `prop:noanchor`.
96	
97	4. Field usage: correct. `Λ^NC` is normalized by `q=q_line=q_code=q_chal` in `emca` (`thm:normalform`); slopes `z=P(α)/B∈F_q`. The reserve uses `q_gen=q_D` via `τ*(ρ,q_gen)` and the entropy condition, transferred under `ρ→ρ+1/n`, `σ→σ−1` (asymptotically unchanged). The `2^-128` target enters as `Λ_1/q≤|L_w|/q`: beating it on the safe side is exactly `|L_w|≤2^{-128}q`, i.e. a polynomial aperiodic list over a large field — the `conj:final-locator` content. I did not conflate the simple binomial reserve with the corrected `τ*`/quotient reserve.
98	
99	5. Reductions of the numerator. Quotient/periodic: yes, charged — periodic `w` (dimension‑`(k+1)` quotient cores) give the superpolynomial part, separated into `2^{(β/H)Q_H(η)}` (`rem:aper`), excluded from `Λ^aper`. Contained incidences: removed by noncontainment, but here noncontainment is *automatic*, so it removes nothing — the `t=1`,`B=1` cloud is the full eval‑image, no slope is lost to containment. Same‑slope collisions: these are exactly the `eval_α` collisions `P(α)=P'(α)`; they are the *only* mechanism that can push `|Z|` below `|L_w|`, and the adversary defeats them by choosing `α∈F∖D` avoiding the `≤k` roots of each pairwise difference whenever `q` is large. Affine color normalization: `B≠1` rescales `z↦z/B` and a degree‑`≤k` codeword added to `w` translates `z↦z+P_0(α)`; both are count‑invariant bijections, so they do not reduce the numerator.
100	
101	6. This PLAN converts to PROOF via L‑CYCLE96‑ARBITRARY‑WORD‑NORM‑SIEVE (equivalently `conj:final-locator` at dim `k+1`), which makes the aperiodic `t=1` cloud `≤n^B`; it converts to COUNTERPACKET via an aperiodic, non‑quotient‑periodic `w` whose dimension‑`(k+1)` list is superpolynomial and survives `eval_α` — which would simultaneously refute `conj:final-locator`. Either resolves the headline wall, and both now point at the same object: arbitrary‑word locator collisions, not anchored secants.

I read `readme.md`/director state, `tex/slackMCA_v3.tex` (`def:mca`, `def:residue`, `thm:normalform`, `prop:noanchor`, `prob:perfiber`, `conj:B`, `conj:final-locator`/`conj:final-mca`, the slack calculus `thm:exactslack`/`lem:tlocator`/`thm:slackt`, the list side `def:locator-fiber`/`prop:arb-fiber`/`thm:qcore`/`cor:upstairs-poly`, and the Galois sieve `lem:galois-amp`/`thm:no-collision`/`prop:qfloor`), the Cycle94 audit and raw response, and the Cycle94 toy checker. Below is the Cycle95 result.

## 1. Executive verdict and confidence

I did not find an independent elementary proof of the `t=1` arbitrary‑anchor bound, and I can show cleanly why one should not exist by the proposed route. What I can bank is an **exact reduction**: the `t=1`, `E=X−α`, `B=1` residue cloud is, slope for slope, the evaluation‑at‑`α` image of the **dimension‑`(k+1)` arbitrary‑word locator list** of `w`. Three consequences are unconditional and checkable: (i) noncontainment is automatic in this stratum; (ii) the bad‑slope set is *exactly* `{P(α) : P∈List(RS[F,D,k+1], w) at agreement k+σ}`; (iii) hence the cloud has size `≤ |List|`. The headline finding is therefore that `L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-PERFIBER-COLLISION-T1` is **not a new wall** — it collapses onto the already‑isolated arbitrary‑word list local limit `conj:final-locator` (at dimension `k+1`), so the `t=1` MCA wall and the list wall are the same wall.

Confidence: high on the reduction and on automatic noncontainment (elementary, and exactly what the Cycle94 toy checker already computes). High on the route cut (it is `prop:noanchor` made quantitative). Moderate‑to‑high that the only place a genuinely `t=1`‑specific theorem could beat `conj:final-locator` is the eval‑at‑`α` collapse, which I argue the adversary defeats by choosing `α` whenever `q` is large.

## 2. Exact `t=1` theorem in source‑valid notation

Fix the slope field `F=F_q` with `q=q_line=q_code=q_chal`, `C=RS[F,D,k]`, `D≤F^×` smooth generated‑field of order `n`, `k=ρn`, `s=k+σ`, `δ=1−(k+σ)/n=1−ρ−η`, `η=σ/n`. Take the datum `(E,B,w)=(X−α, 1, w)` with `α∈F∖D`, `w:D→F` arbitrary (`def:residue`, `t=1`). Write `L_w := List(RS[F,D,k+1], w, δ) = {P∈F[X] : deg P≤k, |{x∈D:P(x)=w(x)}|≥k+σ}` (the dimension‑`(k+1)` list of `w`; `def:locator-fiber`, `prop:arb-fiber` with `k→k+1`).

L‑CYCLE95‑ANCHORED‑T1‑EVAL‑LIST (bankable, exact).
```
(a) Automatic noncontainment: for every witness with |S|>k, the direction word
    g = -B/E = -1/(X-α) is NOT explained by any G∈F_{<k}[X] on S; so every
    (k+σ)-agreement witness is noncontained.
(b) Exact slope identity: the set of support-wise MCA-bad slopes of the line
    y_z = w/(X-α) - z/(X-α) at radius δ is EXACTLY
        Z = { P(α) : P ∈ L_w }.
(c) Count: Λ^NC_{1,δ}((X-α,1,w)) = |Z| = |eval_α(L_w)| ≤ |L_w|.
(d) For a general nonzero constant B, z = P(α)/B; the count is identical
    (affine color rescaling).
```

The exact source‑valid `t=1` RCUT statement is then conditional, and it is the cleanest sharp replacement:
```
THEOREM (t=1 anchored RCUT, conditional on conj:final-locator at dim k+1).
For aperiodic w (w not in a dimension-(k+1) quotient-core class, rem:aper),
σ≥Cn/log n and σ·log₂ q_gen ≥ (1+ε)·log₂ C(n,k+σ):
        Λ^NC_{1,1-ρ-η}((X-α,1,w)) ≤ |L_w| ≤ n^{B}.
The quotient-periodic w contribute the separated 2^{(β/H)Q_H(η)} term, exactly
as in conj:B / rem:aper, transferred through ρ→ρ+1/n.
```

## 3. Proof of the bankable lemma, and the obstruction to the strong claim

Proof of (a). A witness needs `G∈F_{<k}[X]` with `(x−α)G(x)=−1` for `x∈S`. Then `(X−α)G(X)+1` has degree `≤k` and vanishes on `S`, `|S|=k+σ>k`; a nonzero polynomial of degree `≤k` cannot have `>k` roots, so `(X−α)G+1≡0`, forcing `(X−α)∣1` — impossible. So `g=−1/(X−α)` is never contained on a `>k` support; noncontainment holds for free. (This is exactly why the simple‑pole direction is a *genuine* line, `def:mca`(ii) always active; it uses only `B≠0`, `deg B<t=1`, so it holds for every `t=1` datum.)

Proof of (b),(c). A witness is `(Q_z,S_z)` with `deg Q_z<k+t=k+1`, `Q_z≡zB≡z (mod X−α)` i.e. `Q_z(α)=z`, and `Q_z=w` on `S_z`, `|S_z|≥k+σ`. So `Q_z` is a degree‑`≤k` polynomial agreeing with `w` on `≥k+σ` points: `Q_z∈L_w`, and `z=Q_z(α)`. Conversely any `P∈L_w` yields the bad slope `z=P(α)` with witness `(P, {P=w})` (noncontained by (a)). Distinct slopes force distinct `P` (since `z=P(α)`), so `Z=eval_α(L_w)` exactly, and `|Z|≤|L_w|`. ∎ This is precisely the computation in `cycle94_anchored_secant_toy_check.py`: for `t=1` it interpolates `w` on each support, keeps it iff `deg<k+1`, and reads the slope as `q_poly mod (X−α)=Q_S(α)`.

Why the strong (unconditional) claim is blocked. By (b)–(c), bounding the aperiodic `t=1` cloud is bounding the **arbitrary‑word** dimension‑`(k+1)` list. Three facts pin this as genuinely the open list problem, not an easier secant problem:

- We are *beyond Johnson*: radius `1−ρ−η` with `η=Θ(1/log n)` and `ρ+η<√ρ` for fixed `ρ`, so combinatorial list‑decoding bounds do not apply; quotient cores (`thm:qcore`) give superpolynomial lists here, exactly the periodic part we must charge.
- `prop:noanchor`: an arbitrary anchor `w` admits no word‑free/characteristic‑zero condition — `f=w/E` is an arbitrary word — so every prime‑averaging device (`thm:diag`, `prop:qfloor`, `thm:stable`, `thm:no-collision`) is structurally inapplicable. The monomial sieve `thm:no-collision` used coefficients in `{−1,0,1}` (`|N(G(ξ))|≤(2n)^{n/2M}`); the arbitrary‑word lift `U∈Z[ζ][X]` has *unbounded* archimedean coefficients, so the norm bound `‖h‖₁≤2s` of `prop:qfloor` has no analogue. This is the exact inhomogeneous obstruction the Cycle94 plan flagged, now localized to the missing archimedean bound.
- Cycle94 secant route: distinct slopes give codewords `P_i` with `|S_i∩S_j|≤k+t−1=k` (the difference vanishes on the common agreement and is degree `≤k`); the `S_i` are a constant‑weight code of distance `2σ`, which is GV‑exponential — so the secant bound alone yields nothing, and the extra fact "`P_i−P_j` does not vanish at `α`" does not improve the count. Route cut re‑confirmed at `t=1`.

Can the anchor be removed / normalized / charged? Removed: no — `w=0` gives `Q_z≡0` and `z=0` only (`Λ=1`); the entire content is in `w`. Normalized: yes but harmlessly — adding a degree‑`≤k` codeword to `w` translates all slopes by `P_0(α)` (count‑invariant), and `B≠1` only rescales `z↦z/B`; so only the coset `w+RS[F,D,k+1]` (the syndrome) matters, still an arbitrary high‑degree word. Charged: the quotient‑periodic `w` (dimension‑`(k+1)` quotient cores) are separated into the `2^{(β/H)Q_H(η)}` term exactly as `rem:aper`/`conj:B`, leaving the aperiodic `n^{1+o(1)}` target.

Is there a counterpacket? A source‑valid `t=1` counterpacket (aperiodic `w`, stable superpolynomial cloud, not quotient‑periodic) would be, by (b)–(c), an aperiodic superpolynomial dimension‑`(k+1)` list whose `eval_α` image stays superpolynomial — i.e. a refutation of `conj:final-locator`, the paper's expected positive theorem. The only known superpolynomial source is quotient cores, which are periodic and excluded. So no counterpacket is available here; producing one would be a far bigger event (killing the list conjecture), and I do not have a mechanism for it.

## 4. Verification requirements

- (a),(b),(c) are reproducible by the existing `cycle94_anchored_secant_toy_check.py` restricted to `t=1`: it already computes `slope = Q_S(α)` and groups supports by slope. Add two assertions: (i) for every random `(α,w)` over toy `F_p`, the set of bad slopes equals `{P(α)}` over the enumerated dimension‑`(k+1)` codeword list of `w` (re‑interpolate, dedupe by codeword), confirming the exact identity and `|Z|≤|codeword list|`; (ii) confirm noncontainment fires for *every* support (no `G` exists), i.e. the `t=1` cloud loses nothing to containment.
- The conditional theorem needs nothing new beyond `conj:final-locator`; to test the eval‑collapse question, extend `scripts/run_frontier.py` to sweep `α∈F∖D` for fixed superpolynomial quotient‑core `w` and record `|eval_α(list)|` vs `|list|` as `q` grows — this measures whether the projection can ever rescue an exponential list (it should not, for large `q`).

## 5. Next exact lemma / construction

The single statement that converts the `t=1` (and, with the dimension shift absorbed, the general) arbitrary‑anchor RCUT from PLAN to PROOF is the arbitrary‑word generalization of `thm:no-collision`:

L‑CYCLE96‑ARBITRARY‑WORD‑NORM‑SIEVE (target).
```
Let p≡1 (mod n), H≅μ_n, U∈Z[ζ_n][X] a lift of an arbitrary word w:H→F_p,
deg U<n, dimension k+1. For (k+σ)-supports S,S' with deg(U mod L_S),
deg(U mod L_{S'})≤k and P_S≡P_{S'} (mod 𝔭): if σ≥Cn/log n and
p>exp(C·n log n/σ), then either S△S' is quotient-periodic (a union of cosets
of a subgroup of order M>σ), or P_S≡P_{S'} over Z[ζ].
```
The crux is exactly the missing archimedean control: replace the `{−1,0,1}`‑coefficient bound `‖h‖₁≤2s` (valid only for monomial‑prefix `U_c=X^s+…`) by a bound that survives an arbitrary lift `U`, or by a genuine fixed‑prime local‑limit substitute (the `rem:galois-limit` last `log n` factor). The smallest honest milestone toward it: prove the **eval‑collapse never helps** in the large‑field regime — i.e., for `q≥` a suitable polynomial threshold, `|eval_α(L_w)|=Θ(|L_w|)` for adversarial `α` — which shows the `t=1` cloud cannot be polynomially small unless `L_w` already is, closing the equivalence and proving that no `t=1`‑specific shortcut exists.

Dependency DAG for Cycle95:
```
thm:normalform ── Λ^NC at one datum (E,B,w)
        │  t=1, E=X-α, B=1
        ▼
L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-T1-EVAL-LIST  (PROVED)
   bad slopes = eval_α(dimension-(k+1) arbitrary-word list of w)
   noncontainment automatic; secant/norm-sieve route CUT (=arbitrary-word list)
        │  collapses onto
        ▼
conj:final-locator (dim k+1)  ⟸  L-CYCLE96-ARBITRARY-WORD-NORM-SIEVE (open)
        │                         crux: archimedean control of arbitrary lift
        ▼
RCUT = W-OFFICIAL-MCA-MATCHING-UPPER-ARBITRARY-ANCHOR-RESIDUE-CLOUD
```

## Self‑audit (six required questions)

1. Proved: the exact identity `Λ^NC_{1,δ}((X−α,1,w)) = |eval_α(L_w)| ≤ |L_w|` and automatic noncontainment for every `t=1` datum (`B≠0`, `deg B<1`). Not proved: the `t=1` polynomial bound itself, in any nontrivial field regime — it is reduced to, and is morally as hard as, `conj:final-locator` at dimension `k+1`.

2. Official‑prize‑relevant in direction (it is about `emca` over `thm:normalform`, `q_chal=q_line=q`, target `2^-128`), but the deliverable is a reduction lemma plus a route cut, not a finite counterpacket and not a positive threshold certificate. It is upper‑side progress that *identifies the wall*, not a finished safe‑side theorem.

3. First line that could fail: none in L‑CYCLE95‑ANCHORED‑T1‑EVAL‑LIST (unconditional, degree count). In the conditional theorem the first failure point is `conj:final-locator` itself; concretely, the archimedean step of `prop:qfloor`/`thm:no-collision` (`‖h‖₁≤2s`) has no analogue for an arbitrary lift `U`, exactly `prop:noanchor`.

4. Field usage: correct. `Λ^NC` is normalized by `q=q_line=q_code=q_chal` in `emca` (`thm:normalform`); slopes `z=P(α)/B∈F_q`. The reserve uses `q_gen=q_D` via `τ*(ρ,q_gen)` and the entropy condition, transferred under `ρ→ρ+1/n`, `σ→σ−1` (asymptotically unchanged). The `2^-128` target enters as `Λ_1/q≤|L_w|/q`: beating it on the safe side is exactly `|L_w|≤2^{-128}q`, i.e. a polynomial aperiodic list over a large field — the `conj:final-locator` content. I did not conflate the simple binomial reserve with the corrected `τ*`/quotient reserve.

5. Reductions of the numerator. Quotient/periodic: yes, charged — periodic `w` (dimension‑`(k+1)` quotient cores) give the superpolynomial part, separated into `2^{(β/H)Q_H(η)}` (`rem:aper`), excluded from `Λ^aper`. Contained incidences: removed by noncontainment, but here noncontainment is *automatic*, so it removes nothing — the `t=1`,`B=1` cloud is the full eval‑image, no slope is lost to containment. Same‑slope collisions: these are exactly the `eval_α` collisions `P(α)=P'(α)`; they are the *only* mechanism that can push `|Z|` below `|L_w|`, and the adversary defeats them by choosing `α∈F∖D` avoiding the `≤k` roots of each pairwise difference whenever `q` is large. Affine color normalization: `B≠1` rescales `z↦z/B` and a degree‑`≤k` codeword added to `w` translates `z↦z+P_0(α)`; both are count‑invariant bijections, so they do not reduce the numerator.

6. This PLAN converts to PROOF via L‑CYCLE96‑ARBITRARY‑WORD‑NORM‑SIEVE (equivalently `conj:final-locator` at dim `k+1`), which makes the aperiodic `t=1` cloud `≤n^B`; it converts to COUNTERPACKET via an aperiodic, non‑quotient‑periodic `w` whose dimension‑`(k+1)` list is superpolynomial and survives `eval_α` — which would simultaneously refute `conj:final-locator`. Either resolves the headline wall, and both now point at the same object: arbitrary‑word locator collisions, not anchored secants.

# Cycle 96 Prompt: Arbitrary-Word Norm Sieve

You are working on RS-MCA / Proximity Prize research.

No Internet. Take all the time to reason you need. Use MAX reasoning.

Try to fully solve the problem. If you cannot fully solve it, progress it as
much as possible. Do you see a route to a full solve? If yes, what is the next
exact lemma or construction?

Before finalizing, do a self-audit.

Explicitly answer:

1. What exact implication did I prove, and what exact implication did I not prove?
2. Is the result official-prize-relevant, or only a finite/model/research certificate?
3. What is the first line in the reduction chain where the theorem could fail?
4. Are q_gen, q_line, q_code, q_chal, and the 2^-128 target being used correctly?
5. Could quotient/periodic structure, contained incidences, same-slope collisions, or affine color normalization reduce the claimed numerator?
6. If my answer is a PLAN, what exact theorem/checker/counterpacket would convert it into PROOF or COUNTERPACKET?

Do not hide behind broad language. Give the exact next lemma, exact missing
hypothesis, or exact counterexample mechanism.

## Current State

Cycle95 banked:

```text
L-CYCLE95-ANCHORED-T1-EVAL-LIST
```

For `E=X-alpha`, `B=1`, `alpha notin D`, and arbitrary anchor `w`, the
support-wise noncontained bad slopes of:

```text
y_z(x) = w(x)/(x-alpha) - z/(x-alpha)
```

are exactly:

```text
{ P(alpha) : deg P <= k, |{x in D : P(x)=w(x)}| >= k+sigma }.
```

Noncontainment is automatic. Therefore the `t=1` anchored MCA cloud collapses
onto the arbitrary-word list problem at dimension `k+1`.

Cycle95 cuts any attempt to solve the `t=1` wall without solving the
arbitrary-word locator/list wall or producing a counterpacket to it.

## Target

Attack the exact new wall:

```text
L-CYCLE96-ARBITRARY-WORD-NORM-SIEVE
```

The monomial proof uses Galois/norm sieve estimates with small coefficients,
ultimately bounding norms using a small `l1` coefficient bound. For arbitrary
anchor words, a lift:

```text
U in Z[zeta_n][X]
```

can have uncontrolled archimedean coefficients. This is the first line where
the Cycle95 conditional reduction can fail.

Prove, kill, or sharply reduce:

```text
Arbitrary-word locator/list local limit:
after quotient-periodic cores are charged, the number of degree-<=k
polynomials agreeing with an arbitrary aperiodic word w on k+sigma points is
n^{O(1)} above corrected reserve.
```

Equivalently, prove a replacement for the monomial norm sieve that survives an
arbitrary lift `U`, or construct a source-valid aperiodic non-quotient
arbitrary word with a superpolynomial list.

## Required Tasks

1. Read the normal-form/list source definitions and Cycle95 audit.
2. State the exact arbitrary-word list theorem needed at dimension `k+1`.
3. Identify whether any normalization of `w` gives bounded archimedean data.
4. Try to prove an arbitrary-word norm sieve, fixed-prime local limit, or
   alternative collision bound.
5. If the theorem is false, output a source-valid counterpacket mechanism:
   aperiodic `w`, smooth generated-field domain, superpolynomial list, not
   charged by quotient-periodic cores.
6. If no proof/counterpacket is possible, name the smallest exact sublemma
   below this wall.

## Source Files To Read

Use the mounted project source. Read:

```text
readme.md
agents.md
tex/slackMCA_v3.tex
experimental/notes/m1/m1_cycle95_anchored_perfiber_t1_audit.md
experimental/notes/m1/cycle95_anchored_perfiber_t1_raw/response.md
experimental/scripts/cycle95_t1_eval_list_check.py
```

## Required Output Format

Start with one of:

```text
PROOF
COUNTERPACKET
BANKABLE_LEMMA
ROUTE_CUT
EXACT_NEW_WALL
AUDIT
PLAN
```

Then give:

1. Executive verdict and confidence.
2. Exact theorem/counterpacket/route cut.
3. Proof or obstruction.
4. Verification requirements.
5. Next exact lemma or construction.

Do not return a lower-side quotient-floor construction. This is the arbitrary
word upper-side wall.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Finite sanity check for Cycle95's t=1 eval-list reduction.
3	
4	This script is not a proof. It verifies on small fields that for
5	E = X - alpha, B = 1, and arbitrary anchor w, the bad slopes are exactly
6	{P(alpha) : deg P <= k and P agrees with w on at least k+sigma points}.
7	It also checks that the t=1 direction is noncontained on every witness support.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	from itertools import combinations, product
13	from random import Random
14	
15	
16	def trim(poly: list[int]) -> list[int]:
17	    while len(poly) > 1 and poly[-1] == 0:
18	        poly.pop()
19	    return poly
20	
21	
22	def add(a: list[int], b: list[int], p: int) -> list[int]:
23	    n = max(len(a), len(b))
24	    out = [0] * n
25	    for i in range(n):
26	        out[i] = ((a[i] if i < len(a) else 0) + (b[i] if i < len(b) else 0)) % p
27	    return trim(out)
28	
29	
30	def mul(a: list[int], b: list[int], p: int) -> list[int]:
31	    out = [0] * (len(a) + len(b) - 1)
32	    for i, av in enumerate(a):
33	        for j, bv in enumerate(b):
34	            out[i + j] = (out[i + j] + av * bv) % p
35	    return trim(out)
36	
37	
38	def scale(a: list[int], c: int, p: int) -> list[int]:
39	    return trim([(c * x) % p for x in a])
40	
41	
42	def eval_poly(poly: list[int], x: int, p: int) -> int:
43	    acc = 0
44	    for coeff in reversed(poly):
45	        acc = (acc * x + coeff) % p
46	    return acc
47	
48	
49	def interpolate(points: list[tuple[int, int]], p: int) -> list[int]:
50	    out = [0]
51	    for i, (xi, yi) in enumerate(points):
52	        num = [1]
53	        den = 1
54	        for j, (xj, _) in enumerate(points):
55	            if i == j:
56	                continue
57	            num = mul(num, [(-xj) % p, 1], p)
58	            den = den * ((xi - xj) % p) % p
59	        out = add(out, scale(num, yi * pow(den, -1, p), p), p)
60	    return trim(out)
61	
62	
63	def all_polys_deg_le(k: int, p: int):
64	    for coeffs in product(range(p), repeat=k + 1):
65	        yield trim(list(coeffs))
66	
67	
68	def noncontained_t1(support: tuple[int, ...], alpha: int, k: int, p: int) -> bool:
69	    target = [(-pow((x - alpha) % p, -1, p)) % p for x in support]
70	    for coeffs in product(range(p), repeat=k):
71	        g = trim(list(coeffs))
72	        if all(eval_poly(g, x, p) == y for x, y in zip(support, target)):
73	            return False
74	    return True
75	
76	
77	def scan_case(p: int, n: int, k: int, sigma: int, alpha: int, seed: int) -> dict:
78	    domain = list(range(n))
79	    assert alpha not in domain
80	    rng = Random(seed)
81	    w = [rng.randrange(p) for _ in domain]
82	    s = k + sigma
83	
84	    bad_slopes = set()
85	    supports_checked = 0
86	    for support in combinations(domain, s):
87	        q_poly = interpolate([(x, w[x]) for x in support], p)
88	        if len(q_poly) - 1 <= k:
89	            supports_checked += 1
90	            assert noncontained_t1(support, alpha, k, p)
91	            bad_slopes.add(eval_poly(q_poly, alpha, p))
92	
93	    list_polys = []
94	    eval_slopes = set()
95	    for poly in all_polys_deg_le(k, p):
96	        agreement = sum(1 for x in domain if eval_poly(poly, x, p) == w[x])
97	        if agreement >= s:
98	            list_polys.append(tuple(poly))
99	            eval_slopes.add(eval_poly(poly, alpha, p))
100	
101	    assert bad_slopes == eval_slopes, (bad_slopes, eval_slopes)
102	    return {
103	        "p": p,
104	        "n": n,
105	        "k": k,
106	        "sigma": sigma,
107	        "alpha": alpha,
108	        "seed": seed,
109	        "bad_slope_count": len(bad_slopes),
110	        "list_size": len(list_polys),
111	        "supports_checked": supports_checked,
112	        "eval_collisions": len(list_polys) - len(eval_slopes),
113	        "passed": True,
114	    }
115	
116	
117	def main() -> None:
118	    cases = [
119	        (11, 7, 2, 2, 9, range(30)),
120	        (13, 8, 2, 2, 11, range(30)),
121	        (17, 8, 3, 2, 13, range(10)),
122	    ]
123	    reports = []
124	    for p, n, k, sigma, alpha, seeds in cases:
125	        for seed in seeds:
126	            reports.append(scan_case(p, n, k, sigma, alpha, seed))
127	    print("cycle95 t=1 eval-list sanity check")
128	    print(f"cases_checked: {len(reports)}")
129	    print(f"total_bad_slopes_seen: {sum(r['bad_slope_count'] for r in reports)}")
130	    print(f"total_list_polys_seen: {sum(r['list_size'] for r in reports)}")
131	    for item in reports[:6]:
132	        print(item)
133	    print("PASS")
134	
135	
136	if __name__ == "__main__":
137	    main()
138

content.thinking:
I'm recognizing that this is fundamentally a list-decoding problem for Reed-Solomon codes operating beyond the Johnson bound—specifically asking how many low-degree polynomials can agree with an arbitrary word on a sufficiently large number of points. The key parameters are the agreement radius δ = 1 - ρ - η, where ρ represents the code rate and η captures the relative agreement threshold.

The critical insight is that while the Johnson bound would predict a polynomial-sized list when agreement exceeds √ρ, here we're operating at ρ + η which is substantially smaller, suggesting the list could potentially be superpolynomial. The real question is whether, after accounting for quotient-periodic cores, every aperiodic word still yields a polynomial-sized list—and this appears to be an open problem requiring careful analysis of the collision-avoidance machinery and the Galois sieve framework.

120	
121	\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=2em]
122	\item \emph{Exact slack calculus.}  The bad-slope set of the canonical slack line $x^{k+T}+zx^k$ is identified exactly as a multi-symmetric image of the evaluation set (\cref{thm:exactslack}).  The quotient locator (\cref{lem:tlocator}) and the coset-collapse proposition (\cref{prop:collapse}) explain why subgroup-symmetric slack witnesses reduce to a one-condition ladder.  Cyclotomic and digit rigidity (\cref{thm:rigidcyclo,thm:rigidfermat}) show that the remaining asymmetric witnesses are genuinely modular rather than characteristic-zero phenomena.
123	
124	\item \emph{Failure mechanisms.}  The Dias da Silva--Hamidoune theorem gives certified slack-one failure at the square-root barrier (\cref{cor:certified}), while the two-moment construction gives full coverage of $(e_1,e_2)$ under explicit square-root hypotheses (\cref{thm:twomoment,cor:twomomentmca}).  The dyadic descent theorem is retained with all divisibility assumptions made explicit (\cref{thm:descent}).  The free-pool ladder is stated conditionally on a precise subgroup exponential-sum estimate (\cref{thm:ladder}); where the cited estimates supply that estimate, the stated ranges follow, but the theorem is not used as a black-box proof of stronger fixed-prime equidistribution.
125	
126	\item \emph{Characteristic-zero rigidity and direction arithmetic.}  The inverse quotient theorem (\cref{thm:rigidity}) proves that vanishing at dyadic frequencies forces quotient invariance.  Consequently, zero-base non-dyadic directions have no characteristic-zero nonzero slopes (\cref{thm:directions}), while dyadic directions reduce to lower-level direction-one problems.  The earlier all-bases non-dyadic theorem is replaced by the Newton-obstruction criterion of \cref{thm:allbasesdir}; arbitrary bases require extra equations before the rigidity theorem can be applied.
127	
128	\item \emph{Positive density, quotient floors, and stable results.}  At deepest-level slack $\sigma$, the correct normalization is
129	\[
130	        \ceff=\frac{\sigma\log_2 n}{n}.
131	\]
132	With this bookkeeping, the canonical line has a no-threshold descent floor of order $\rho(1-\rho)(\log_2n/\ceff)^2$ at every prime and a density ceiling for at least half the admissible primes in explicit windows (\cref{thm:diag}).  The quotient-exact floor of \cref{prop:qfloor} upgrades the floor to $n^{\beta(\rho)/\ceff(1-o(1))}$ at every prime above the quotient norm threshold.  Every monomial base in the canonical direction obeys the same quotient-class cap, with a sharpened collision term (\cref{thm:allbases}).  In the stable range $p>(2s)^{n/2}$, the canonical-line and zero-base direction predictions are exact at every prime --- exactness is claimed only for these proved strata, not for arbitrary bases --- and the closed-form canonical count remains valid (\cref{thm:stable,thm:exactcount}).
133	
134	\item \emph{Boundary and conjecture.}  The exact normal form (\cref{thm:normalform}) identifies the residue-line packing number that equals MCA error after maximizing over line data.  The denominator-closure statement is kept as a coordinate normal form, not a quantitative bound (\cref{thm:closure}).  The quotient-profile lower bound (\cref{thm:qnecessity}) calibrates the necessary quotient reserve for polynomial MCA bounds at large primes, while the final conjecture remains a per-fiber packing conjecture with a separate conditional failure half (\cref{conj:B}).
135	\end{enumerate}
136	
137	
138	The merged theorem package, with proof status, is as follows.
139	
140	\begin{theorem}[Integrated theorem package, with status]\label{thm:integrated-package}
141	For smooth multiplicative domains $H$ of order $n=2^m$ at fixed rate $\rho$:
142	\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)},leftmargin=2.4em]
143	\item \textup{(Proved.)}  Below the entropy scale $\taustar(\rho,q)$, explicit monomial-prefix received words have exponentially many nearby codewords (\cref{thm:pigeonhole,cor:entropy-lower}).
144	\item \textup{(Proved.)}  Independently of $q$, quotient cores produce at least $\binom{n/M-1}{k/M}$ codewords at agreement $k+\sigma$ whenever $M\mid k$, $k/M\le n/M-1$, and $\sigma<M$; consequently, at the deployed dyadic rates (and along any scale with $M\mid k$), polynomial-in-$n$ list decoding requires $\sigma=\Omega(n/\log n)$, and the one-scale ambient-$q$ entropy-gap prediction is false even over prime fields (\cref{thm:qcore,cor:one-scale-false}).
145	\item \textup{(Proved.)}  Over characteristic zero, equal prefix moments among subsets of $\mu_n$ are exactly quotient-periodic; characteristic-zero prefix fibers are polynomial once $\sigma\ge Cn/\log n$ (\cref{thm:upstairs,cor:upstairs-poly}).
146	\item \textup{(Proved.)}  If $p\equiv1\pmod n$ and $p>\exp(C_1n\log(2n)/\sigma)$, every finite-field monomial-prefix collision is quotient-periodic; monomial-prefix received words have polynomial lists at $\sigma\ge C_0n/\log_2n$ for split primes in the quasi-polynomial range $p>\exp(O((\log n)^2))$ (\cref{thm:no-collision,cor:quasipoly-upper}).
147	\item \textup{(Proved or conditional, as marked.)}  For MCA, the canonical-line bad-slope set at slack $T$ is exactly the multi-symmetric image $\mathcal B_T$ (\cref{thm:exactslack}), and subgroup-symmetric witnesses collapse onto the one-condition ladder (\cref{prop:collapse}).  Unconditional failure mechanisms are the certified slack-one rung (\cref{cor:certified}), two-moment fullness at the root barrier (\cref{thm:twomoment,cor:twomomentmca}), and dyadic descent (\cref{thm:descent}); the polynomial-range free-pool ladder is conditional on a precise subgroup exponential-sum input (\cref{thm:ladder}).  The quotient-exact floor survives at every prime above the quotient norm threshold (\cref{prop:qfloor}), the quotient profile is necessary for polynomial MCA slope bounds at large primes (\cref{thm:qnecessity}), and the deployed-rate challenge cap holds below the per-rate prime-field reach (\cref{prop:prize}).  All-line positive MCA is equivalent, up to the tangent floor, to residue-line packing (\cref{thm:normalform}).
148	\item \textup{(Proved, with imported black boxes as marked.)}  Transfers and caps: MCA-bad slopes of $B$-valued lines over an extension $\F\supseteq B$ are confined to $B$, deflating the prime-field witness theory to density at most $|B|/|\F|$ while list obstructions persist (\cref{thm:subfield,cor:deflate,cor:decouple}); the canonical locator stratum on $\F_2$-affine domains is confined to a single additive coset (\cref{prop:additive}); quotient cores and the slack-one stratum transfer to Dickson--Chebyshev and circle-group $x$-coordinate domains, with error one at Mersenne-$31$ parameters for those Reed--Solomon constituents (\cref{thm:chebqcore,prop:chebslack,cor:circle}); the $\{2,3\}$-smooth relation module gives the mixed-radix rigidity input, and arbitrary-base slabs reduce to at most $C(\sigma,s)$ torsion-coset template families under the stated effective torsion-coset import (\cref{thm:23rigidity,thm:templates,cor:reszero}); and, conditional on the imported list-to-agreement conversion of \cite{CS25}, smooth-domain challenge codes at the deployed rates with $2^{11}\mid n$, $n\le q/2$, $k\le2^{40}$, and $q\le2^{256}$ have $\emca>2^{-42}$ at gap $2^{-11}$ (\cref{thm:cap,cor:captiers}); the sharpened form --- gap $2^{-9}$, error $2^{-86}$, no field-size condition --- is \cite{UniversalCap}.
149	\item \textup{(Conjectural.)}  The full polynomial-field positive list theorem and the floor-corrected all-line MCA theorem are isolated as finite-field local-limit statements with quotient exceptions explicitly separated (\cref{conj:prefix-local,conj:arbitrary-local,conj:listprofile,conj:B,conj:final-locator,conj:final-mca}).
150	\end{enumerate}
151	\end{theorem}
152	
153	\paragraph{Setting and notation.}
154	$p$ is prime ($q$ a general prime power where stated); $D$ or $H\le\F_q^\times$ is a multiplicative subgroup of order $n$, \emph{smooth} meaning $n$ is a power of two; $C=\RS[\F_q,D,k]=\{(P(x))_{x\in D}:\deg P<k\}$ with $k=\rho n$, $\rho\in(0,1)$ fixed.  For a finite set $A$ in a field, $L_A(X)=\prod_{a\in A}(X-a)=X^{|A|}+\sum_{j\ge1}(-1)^je_j(A)X^{|A|-j}$ and $p_j(A)=\sum_{a\in A}a^j$; for $h\ge0$, $h^{\wedge}A$ is the set of sums of $h$ distinct elements of $A$.  $\HH$ is binary entropy.  Divisibility hypotheses ($\rho N\in\Z$, $t\mid N$, $t\mid\rho N$, $\sigma\mid k$, and the like) are stated where they are used.
155	
156	\part{List entropy, quotient cores, and Route 1}
157	
158	\section{List decoding as a locator-fiber problem}\label{sec:fiber}
159	
160	Every received word $y:H\to\F_q$ has a unique interpolant $U\in\F_q[X]$ of degree $<n$ on $H$.  Write $\List(y,\delta)$ for the list of codewords within relative radius $\delta$ of $y$, and $\Lst(C,\delta)=\max_y|\List(y,\delta)|$ for the worst-case list size.  A codeword $P$ agrees with $y$ on a set $S\subseteq H$ if and only if
161	\[
162	        U(X)\equiv P(X)\pmod{L_S(X)}.
163	\]
164	Since $\deg P<k$, this gives the following exact reduction.
165	
166	\begin{definition}[Locator fibers]\label{def:locator-fiber}
167	For $k<s\le n$ and $\deg U<n$, define
168	\[
169	        \Fib_U(s)=\{S\subseteq H:\ |S|=s,\ \deg(U\bmod L_S)<k\}.
170	\]
171	For monomial-prefix data $c=(c_1,\ldots,c_\sigma)\in\F_q^\sigma$, $s=k+\sigma$, define
172	\[
173	        \Phi_\sigma(S)=(e_1(S),\ldots,e_\sigma(S)),
174	        \qquad S\in\binom Hs .
175	\]
176	\end{definition}
177	
178	\begin{proposition}[Arbitrary-word fiber upper bound]\label{prop:arb-fiber}
179	For every received word $y$ with interpolant $U$,
180	\[
181	        |\List(y,1-s/n)|\le |\Fib_U(s)|.
182	\]
183	Consequently
184	\[
185	        \Lst(C,1-s/n)\le \max_{\deg U<n}|\Fib_U(s)|.
186	\]
187	\end{proposition}
188	
189	\begin{proof}
190	Each codeword $P$ agreeing with $y$ on at least $s$ coordinates has an $s$-subset $S$ of its agreement set.  Then $U\equiv P\pmod{L_S}$, so $S\in\Fib_U(s)$.  For a fixed $S$, the residue $U\bmod L_S$ is unique, hence the corresponding $P$ is unique.  Choosing one such $S$ for each listed codeword injects the list into $\Fib_U(s)$.
191	\end{proof}
192	
193	\begin{proposition}[Monomial-prefix words are exact prefix fibers]\label{prop:monomial-fiber}
194	Let $s=k+\sigma$ and
195	\[
196	        U_c(X)=X^s+\sum_{j=1}^{\sigma}(-1)^jc_jX^{s-j}.
197	\]
198	Then the map
199	\[
200	        S\longmapsto U_c-L_S
201	\]
202	is a bijection from $\Phi_\sigma^{-1}(c)$ to the list of degree-$<k$ codewords agreeing with $U_c$ on at least $s$ points.  Thus
203	\[
204	        |\List(U_c,1-s/n)|=|\Phi_\sigma^{-1}(c)|.
205	\]
206	\end{proposition}
207	
208	\begin{proof}
209	If $\Phi_\sigma(S)=c$, then the top $\sigma$ coefficients of $U_c$ and $L_S$ agree, so $P_S=U_c-L_S$ has degree $<s-\sigma=k$.  Also $P_S(x)=U_c(x)$ for $x\in S$.  Conversely, if $P$ has degree $<k$ and agrees with $U_c$ on an $s$-set $S$, then $U_c-P$ is monic of degree $s$ and vanishes exactly on $S$, hence $U_c-P=L_S$, forcing $\Phi_\sigma(S)=c$.  Injectivity follows because $L_S$ determines $S$.
210	\end{proof}
211	
212	Thus the positive half of list decoding can be stated without coding language:
213	\[
214	        \max_{\deg U<n}|\Fib_U(k+\sigma)|\quad\text{should be polynomial above the corrected reserve.}
215	\]
216	
217	\section{Two lower-bound scales}\label{sec:lower}
218	
219	The reference scale for the first mechanism is the classical entropy gap.
220	
221	\begin{definition}[Entropy gap]\label{def:taustar}
222	For $0<\rho<1$ and $q\ge2$, $\taustar(\rho,q)$ is the unique $\tau\in(0,1-\rho)$ with $\tau\log_2q=\HH(\rho+\tau)$; as $q\to\infty$ with $\rho$ fixed, $\taustar(\rho,q)=(\HH(\rho)+o(1))/\log_2q$.
223	\end{definition}
224	
225	\subsection{The entropy pigeonhole scale}\label{subsec:pigeon}
226	
227	\begin{theorem}[Coefficient pigeonhole lower bound]\label{thm:pigeonhole}
228	Let $1\le\sigma\le n-k$ and $s=k+\sigma$.  There exists a monomial-prefix word $U_c$ such that
229	\[
230	        |\List(U_c,1-s/n)|\ge \frac{1}{q^\sigma}\binom ns.
231	\]
232	\end{theorem}
233	
234	\begin{proof}
235	The map $\Phi_\sigma:\binom Hs\to\F_q^\sigma$ has $\binom ns$ inputs and at most $q^\sigma$ outputs.  Some fiber has size at least $q^{-\sigma}\binom ns$.  Apply \Cref{prop:monomial-fiber}.
236	\end{proof}
237	
238	\begin{corollary}[Generated-field pigeonhole]\label{cor:genfield-pigeonhole}
239	If $H\subseteq B$ for a subfield $B\le\F_q$ of size $q_D$, then $\Phi_\sigma$ has image in $B^\sigma$ --- its coordinates $e_j(S)$ are elementary symmetric functions of subsets of $H\subseteq B$ --- so the pigeonhole improves to
240	\[
241	        |\List(U_c,1-s/n)|\ \ge\ \frac{1}{q_D^{\,\sigma}}\binom ns
242	\]
243	for some monomial-prefix word $U_c$ with coefficients in $B$.  This is the form consumed by the necessary half of \cref{conj:listprofile}, which states the entropy floor at the generated field; in locator-fiber form, the same one-line strengthening is the subfield pigeonhole of \cite[Lem.\ ``locator fibers are lists'']{UniversalCap}.
244	\end{corollary}
245	
246	\begin{proof}
247	Identical to \cref{thm:pigeonhole} with $\F_q^\sigma$ replaced by $B^\sigma$: $e_j(S)\in B$ for every $S\subseteq H$, so $\Phi_\sigma$ has at most $q_D^{\,\sigma}$ outputs.
248	\end{proof}
249

\]
With this bookkeeping, the canonical line has a no-threshold descent floor of order $\rho(1-\rho)(\log_2n/\ceff)^2$ at every prime and a density ceiling for at least half the admissible primes in explicit windows (\cref{thm:diag}).  The quotient-exact floor of \cref{prop:qfloor} upgrades the floor to $n^{\beta(\rho)/\ceff(1-o(1))}$ at every prime above the quotient norm threshold.  Every monomial base in the canonical direction obeys the same quotient-class cap, with a sharpened collision term (\cref{thm:allbases}).  In the stable range $p>(2s)^{n/2}$, the canonical-line and zero-base direction predictions are exact at every prime --- exactness is claimed only for these proved strata, not for arbitrary bases --- and the closed-form canonical count remains valid (\cref{thm:stable,thm:exactcount}).

\item \emph{Boundary and conjecture.}  The exact normal form (\cref{thm:normalform}) identifies the residue-line packing number that equals MCA error after maximizing over line data.  The denominator-closure statement is kept as a coordinate normal form, not a quantitative bound (\cref{thm:closure}).  The quotient-profile lower bound (\cref{thm:qnecessity}) calibrates the necessary quotient reserve for polynomial MCA bounds at large primes, while the final conjecture remains a per-fiber packing conjecture with a separate conditional failure half (\cref{conj:B}).
\end{enumerate}


The merged theorem package, with proof status, is as follows.

\begin{theorem}[Integrated theorem package, with status]\label{thm:integrated-package}
For smooth multiplicative domains $H$ of order $n=2^m$ at fixed rate $\rho$:
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textup{(\alph*)},leftmargin=2.4em]
\item \textup{(Proved.)}  Below the entropy scale $\taustar(\rho,q)$, explicit monomial-prefix received words have exponentially many nearby codewords (\cref{thm:pigeonhole,cor:entropy-lower}).
\item \textup{(Proved.)}  Independently of $q$, quotient cores produce at least $\binom{n/M-1}{k/M}$ codewords at agreement $k+\sigma$ whenever $M\mid k$, $k/M\le n/M-1$, and $\sigma<M$; consequently, at the deployed dyadic rates (and along any scale with $M\mid k$), polynomial-in-$n$ list decoding requires $\sigma=\Omega(n/\log n)$, and the one-scale ambient-$q$ entropy-gap prediction is false even over prime fields (\cref{thm:qcore,cor:one-scale-false}).
\item \textup{(Proved.)}  Over characteristic zero, equal prefix moments among subsets of $\mu_n$ are exactly quotient-periodic; characteristic-zero prefix fibers are polynomial once $\sigma\ge Cn/\log n$ (\cref{thm:upstairs,cor:upstairs-poly}).
\item \textup{(Proved.)}  If $p\equiv1\pmod n$ and $p>\exp(C_1n\log(2n)/\sigma)$, every finite-field monomial-prefix collision is quotient-periodic; monomial-prefix received words have polynomial lists at $\sigma\ge C_0n/\log_2n$ for split primes in the quasi-polynomial range $p>\exp(O((\log n)^2))$ (\cref{thm:no-collision,cor:quasipoly-upper}).
\item \textup{(Proved or conditional, as marked.)}  For MCA, the canonical-line bad-slope set at slack $T$ is exactly the multi-symmetric image $\mathcal B_T$ (\cref{thm:exactslack}), and subgroup-symmetric witnesses collapse onto the one-condition ladder (\cref{prop:collapse}).  Unconditional failure mechanisms are the certified slack-one rung (\cref{cor:certified}), two-moment fullness at the root barrier (\cref{thm:twomoment,cor:twomomentmca}), and dyadic descent (\cref{thm:descent}); the polynomial-range free-pool ladder is conditional on a precise subgroup exponential-sum input (\cref{thm:ladder}).  The quotient-exact floor survives at every prime above the quotient norm threshold (\cref{prop:qfloor}), the quotient profile is necessary for polynomial MCA slope bounds at large primes (\cref{thm:qnecessity}), and the deployed-rate challenge cap holds below the per-rate prime-field reach (\cref{prop:prize}).  All-line positive MCA is equivalent, up to the tangent floor, to residue-line packing (\cref{thm:normalform}).
\item \textup{(Proved, with imported black boxes as marked.)}  Transfers and caps: MCA-bad slopes of $B$-valued lines over an extension $\F\supseteq B$ are confined to $B$, deflating the prime-field witness theory to density at most $|B|/|\F|$ while list obstructions persist (\cref{thm:subfield,cor:deflate,cor:decouple}); the canonical locator stratum on $\F_2$-affine domains is confined to a single additive coset (\cref{prop:additive}); quotient cores and the slack-one stratum transfer to Dickson--Chebyshev and circle-group $x$-coordinate domains, with error one at Mersenne-$31$ parameters for those Reed--Solomon constituents (\cref{thm:chebqcore,prop:chebslack,cor:circle}); the $\{2,3\}$-smooth relation module gives the mixed-radix rigidity input, and arbitrary-base slabs reduce to at most $C(\sigma,s)$ torsion-coset template families under the stated effective torsion-coset import (\cref{thm:23rigidity,thm:templates,cor:reszero}); and, conditional on the imported list-to-agreement conversion of \cite{CS25}, smooth-domain challenge codes at the deployed rates with $2^{11}\mid n$, $n\le q/2$, $k\le2^{40}$, and $q\le2^{256}$ have $\emca>2^{-42}$ at gap $2^{-11}$ (\cref{thm:cap,cor:captiers}); the sharpened form --- gap $2^{-9}$, error $2^{-86}$, no field-size condition --- is \cite{UniversalCap}.
\item \textup{(Conjectural.)}  The full polynomial-field positive list theorem and the floor-corrected all-line MCA theorem are isolated as finite-field local-limit statements with quotient exceptions explicitly separated (\cref{conj:prefix-local,conj:arbitrary-local,conj:listprofile,conj:B,conj:final-locator,conj:final-mca}).
\end{enumerate}
\end{theorem}

\paragraph{Setting and notation.}
$p$ is prime ($q$ a general prime power where stated); $D$ or $H\le\F_q^\times$ is a multiplicative subgroup of order $n$, \emph{smooth} meaning $n$ is a power of two; $C=\RS[\F_q,D,k]=\{(P(x))_{x\in D}:\deg P<k\}$ with $k=\rho n$, $\rho\in(0,1)$ fixed.  For a finite set $A$ in a field, $L_A(X)=\prod_{a\in A}(X-a)=X^{|A|}+\sum_{j\ge1}(-1)^je_j(A)X^{|A|-j}$ and $p_j(A)=\sum_{a\in A}a^j$; for $h\ge0$, $h^{\wedge}A$ is the set of sums of $h$ distinct elements of $A$.  $\HH$ is binary entropy.  Divisibility hypotheses ($\rho N\in\Z$, $t\mid N$, $t\mid\rho N$, $\sigma\mid k$, and the like) are stated where they are used.

\part{List entropy, quotient cores, and Route 1}

\section{List decoding as a locator-fiber problem}\label{sec:fiber}

Every received word $y:H\to\F_q$ has a unique interpolant $U\in\F_q[X]$ of degree $<n$ on $H$.  Write $\List(y,\delta)$ for the list of codewords within relative radius $\delta$ of $y$, and $\Lst(C,\delta)=\max_y|\List(y,\delta)|$ for the worst-case list size.  A codeword $P$ agrees with $y$ on a set $S\subseteq H$ if and only if
\[
        U(X)\equiv P(X)\pmod{L_S(X)}.
\]
Since $\deg P<k$, this gives the following exact reduction.

\begin{definition}[Locator fibers]\label{def:locator-fiber}
For $k<s\le n$ and $\deg U<n$, define
\[
        \Fib_U(s)=\{S\subseteq H:\ |S|=s,\ \deg(U\bmod L_S)<k\}.
\]
For monomial-prefix data $c=(c_1,\ldots,c_\sigma)\in\F_q^\sigma$, $s=k+\sigma$, define
\[
        \Phi_\sigma(S)=(e_1(S),\ldots,e_\sigma(S)),
        \qquad S\in\binom Hs .
\]
\end{definition}

\begin{proposition}[Arbitrary-word fiber upper bound]\label{prop:arb-fiber}
For every received word $y$ with interpolant $U$,
\[
        |\List(y,1-s/n)|\le |\Fib_U(s)|.
\]
Consequently
\[
        \Lst(C,1-s/n)\le \max_{\deg U<n}|\Fib_U(s)|.
\]
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
Each codeword $P$ agreeing with $y$ on at least $s$ coordinates has an $s$-subset $S$ of its agreement set.  Then $U\equiv P\pmod{L_S}$, so $S\in\Fib_U(s)$.  For a fixed $S$, the residue $U\bmod L_S$ is unique, hence the corresponding $P$ is unique.  Choosing one such $S$ for each listed codeword injects the list into $\Fib_U(s)$.
\end{proof}

\begin{proposition}[Monomial-prefix words are exact prefix fibers]\label{prop:monomial-fiber}
Let $s=k+\sigma$ and
\[
        U_c(X)=X^s+\sum_{j=1}^{\sigma}(-1)^jc_jX^{s-j}.
\]
Then the map
\[
        S\longmapsto U_c-L_S
\]
is a bijection from $\Phi_\sigma^{-1}(c)$ to the list of degree-$<k$ codewords agreeing with $U_c$ on at least $s$ points.  Thus
\[
        |\List(U_c,1-s/n)|=|\Phi_\sigma^{-1}(c)|.
\]
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
If $\Phi_\sigma(S)=c$, then the top $\sigma$ coefficients of $U_c$ and $L_S$ agree, so $P_S=U_c-L_S$ has degree $<s-\sigma=k$.  Also $P_S(x)=U_c(x)$ for $x\in S$.  Conversely, if $P$ has degree $<k$ and agrees with $U_c$ on an $s$-set $S$, then $U_c-P$ is monic of degree $s$ and vanishes exactly on $S$, hence $U_c-P=L_S$, forcing $\Phi_\sigma(S)=c$.  Injectivity follows because $L_S$ determines $S$.
\end{proof}

Thus the positive half of list decoding can be stated without coding language:
\[
        \max_{\deg U<n}|\Fib_U(k+\sigma)|\quad\text{should be polynomial above the corrected reserve.}
\]

\section{Two lower-bound scales}\label{sec:lower}

The reference scale for the first mechanism is the classical entropy gap.

\begin{definition}[Entropy gap]\label{def:taustar}
For $0<\rho<1$ and $q\ge2$, $\taustar(\rho,q)$ is the unique $\tau\in(0,1-\rho)$ with $\tau\log_2q=\HH(\rho+\tau)$; as $q\to\infty$ with $\rho$ fixed, $\taustar(\rho,q)=(\HH(\rho)+o(1))/\log_2q$.
\end{definition}

\subsection{The entropy pigeonhole scale}\label{subsec:pigeon}

\begin{theorem}[Coefficient pigeonhole lower bound]\label{thm:pigeonhole}
Let $1\le\sigma\le n-k$ and $s=k+\sigma$.  There exists a monomial-prefix word $U_c$ such that
\[
        |\List(U_c,1-s/n)|\ge \frac{1}{q^\sigma}\binom ns.
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
The map $\Phi_\sigma:\binom Hs\to\F_q^\sigma$ has $\binom ns$ inputs and at most $q^\sigma$ outputs.  Some fiber has size at least $q^{-\sigma}\binom ns$.  Apply \Cref{prop:monomial-fiber}.
\end{proof}

\begin{corollary}[Generated-field pigeonhole]\label{cor:genfield-pigeonhole}
If $H\subseteq B$ for a subfield $B\le\F_q$ of size $q_D$, then $\Phi_\sigma$ has image in $B^\sigma$ --- its coordinates $e_j(S)$ are elementary symmetric functions of subsets of $H\subseteq B$ --- so the pigeonhole improves to
\[
        |\List(U_c,1-s/n)|\ \ge\ \frac{1}{q_D^{\,\sigma}}\binom ns
\]
for some monomial-prefix word $U_c$ with coefficients in $B$.  This is the form consumed by the necessary half of \cref{conj:listprofile}, which states the entropy floor at the generated field; in locator-fiber form, the same one-line strengthening is the subfield pigeonhole of \cite[Lem.\ ``locator fibers are lists'']{UniversalCap}.
\end{corollary}

\begin{proof}
Identical to \cref{thm:pigeonhole} with $\F_q^\sigma$ replaced by $B^\sigma$: $e_j(S)\in B$ for every $S\subseteq H$, so $\Phi_\sigma$ has at most $q_D^{\,\sigma}$ outputs.
\end{proof}

249	
250	\begin{corollary}[Failure below the entropy gap]\label{cor:entropy-lower}
251	If $q=2^{o(n)}$, $\sigma=\tau n$, and
252	\[
253	        \sigma\log_2q\le (1-\eps)\log_2\binom n{k+\sigma},
254	\]
255	then some received word has list size at least
256	\[
257	        2^{\eps n\HH(\rho+\tau)-o(n)}.
258	\]
259	In particular, this gives $2^{\Omega(n)}$-size lists for every fixed multiplicative gap below the entropy scale, $\tau\le(1-\eps_0)\taustar(\rho,q)$ with $\eps_0>0$ fixed; for $\eps_0\to0$ the displayed pigeonhole bound still applies but may be subexponential.
260	\end{corollary}
261	
262	\begin{proof}
263	By \cref{thm:pigeonhole}, some monomial-prefix word has list size at least
264	\[
265	        q^{-\sigma}\binom n{k+\sigma}
266	        =2^{\log_2\binom n{k+\sigma}-\sigma\log_2q}
267	        \ge2^{\eps\log_2\binom n{k+\sigma}},
268	\]
269	and $\log_2\binom n{k+\sigma}=n\HH(\rho+\tau)-o(n)$.  For the last sentence, set $h(\tau)=\HH(\rho+\tau)$ and $g(\tau)=\tau\log_2q/h(\tau)$ on $(0,1-\rho)$.  Concavity of $h$ gives $h(\tau)-\tau h'(\tau)\ge h(0)=\HH(\rho)>0$, so $g$ is strictly increasing, and $g(\taustar)=1$ by the definition of $\taustar$.  If $\tau\le(1-\eps_0)\taustar$ with fixed $\eps_0>0$, then concavity again gives $h((1-\eps_0)\taustar)\ge(1-\eps_0)h(\taustar)+\eps_0\HH(\rho)$, whence
270	\[
271	        g(\tau)\le g\big((1-\eps_0)\taustar\big)
272	        \le\frac{(1-\eps_0)h(\taustar)}{(1-\eps_0)h(\taustar)+\eps_0\HH(\rho)}
273	        \le 1-c,
274	        \qquad
275	        c:=\frac{\eps_0\HH(\rho)}{1+\eps_0\HH(\rho)}>0,
276	\]
277	uniformly in $q$, using $h\le1$.  Thus $\sigma\log_2q\le(1-c)\,n\HH(\rho+\tau)$, and the displayed bound is at least $2^{c\,n\HH(\rho+\tau)-o(n)}=2^{\Omega_{\rho,\eps_0}(n)}$: on $[\rho,\rho+\taustar]$ the entropy is at least $\min\{\HH(\rho),\HH(\rho+\taustar)\}$, and $\HH(\rho+\taustar)=\taustar\log_2q$ cannot be small for fixed $\rho$, since otherwise $\rho+\taustar\to1$ would force $\taustar\log_2q\ge\taustar\to1-\rho>0$, a contradiction.
278	\end{proof}
279	
280	\subsection{The quotient-core scale}\label{subsec:qcore}
281	
282	The entropy scale is not the only obstruction.  Smooth subgroups have quotient structure that random domains do not have.
283	
284	\begin{theorem}[Quotient-core list obstruction]\label{thm:qcore}
285	Let $H\le\F_q^\times$ have order $n$.  Let $K\le H$ have order $M$, put $N=n/M$, and assume $M\mid k$ and $k/M\le N-1$.  If $1\le\sigma<M$, then there is a received word $Y:H\to\F_q$ with at least
286	\[
287	        \binom{N-1}{k/M}
288	\]
289	codewords agreeing with $Y$ on $k+\sigma$ coordinates.  Equivalently,
290	\[
291	        \Lst\big(\RS[\F_q,H,k],1-\rho-\sigma/n\big)\ge \binom{n/M-1}{k/M}.
292	\]
293	\end{theorem}
294	
295	\begin{proof}
296	Choose one $K$-coset $C_0\subset H$ and choose $T\subset C_0$ with $|T|=\sigma$.  Let
297	\[
298	        L_T(X)=\prod_{t\in T}(X-t),\qquad Y(X)=X^kL_T(X).
299	\]
300	For every subset $A$ of the quotient $H/K\setminus\{C_0\}$ of size $k/M$, let $U_A\subset H$ be the union of the $K$-cosets in $A$.  Since each $K$-coset is a fiber of $x\mapsto x^M$, its locator has the form $X^M-\alpha$.  Hence
301	\[
302	        L_A(X)=\prod_{x\in U_A}(X-x)=\prod_{\alpha\in A'}(X^M-\alpha)
303	        =X^k+c_1X^{k-M}+c_2X^{k-2M}+\cdots .
304	\]
305	Thus $X^k-L_A(X)$ has degree at most $k-M$.  Define
306	\[
307	        P_A(X)=Y(X)-L_T(X)L_A(X)=L_T(X)(X^k-L_A(X)).
308	\]
309	Since $\deg L_T=\sigma<M$, we have $\deg P_A<k$, so $P_A$ is a codeword.  On $T\cup U_A$, either $L_T$ or $L_A$ vanishes, so $P_A=Y$.  The agreement set has size $\sigma+k$.  Distinct $A$ give distinct locators and distinct codewords.
310	\end{proof}
311	
312	\begin{corollary}[The one-scale ambient-$q$ conjecture is false]\label{cor:one-scale-false}
313	Fix a deployed rate $\rho=2^{-a}$ and $\eps>0$.  There are infinitely many smooth prime-field domains $H\le\F_p^\times$ with $|H|=n$ and
314	\[
315	        p=2^{\Theta(\sqrt n)}
316	\]
317	such that at radius
318	\[
319	        1-\rho-(1+\eps+o(1))\taustar(\rho,p)
320	\]
321	the list size is at least
322	\[
323	        p^{1/(2(1+\eps))-o(1)}=2^{\Theta(\sqrt n)},
324	\]
325	which is superpolynomial in $n$.
326	\end{corollary}
327	
328	\begin{proof}
329	Take $n=2^m$.  By the prime number theorem in arithmetic progressions in a Siegel--Walfisz range \cite{Dav00}, there are primes $p\equiv1\pmod n$ with $\log_2p=\Theta(\sqrt n)$ for all large $n$ along a suitable sequence.  Let $H\le\F_p^\times$ have order $n$.  Since $p$ is prime, there is no proper subfield obstruction.
330	
331	Let $\sigma=\lceil(1+\eps)\taustar(\rho,p)n\rceil$.  Because $\taustar(\rho,p)=(\HH(\rho)+o(1))/\log_2p$, we have $\sigma=\Theta(\sqrt n)$.  Let $M$ be the least power of two larger than $\sigma$.  For large $n$, $M\mid k=\rho n$ and $M\le2\sigma$.  \Cref{thm:qcore} gives
332	\[
333	        L\ge \binom{n/M-1}{k/M}.
334	\]
335	By Stirling,
336	\[
337	        \log_2 L\ge \frac{n}{M}\HH(\rho)-o(n/M)
338	        \ge \frac{\HH(\rho)+o(1)}{2(1+\eps)\taustar(\rho,p)}
339	        =\left(\frac1{2(1+\eps)}-o(1)\right)\log_2p .
340	\]
341	\end{proof}
342	
343	\begin{remark}[Corrected reserve]\label{rem:corrected-reserve}
344	For a list bound $n^B$, the quotient-core family at $\sigma=Cn/\log_2n$ has size about $n^{\HH(\rho)/C}$ on the grid.  Thus a theorem with list bound $n^B$ must have $C\gtrsim \HH(\rho)/B$ at the second scale.  For unspecified polynomial list size, the necessary scale is $\sigma=\Omega(n/\log n)$; for fixed exponent $B$, the constant matters.
345	\end{remark}
346	
347	\begin{definition}[Quotient-core profile]\label{def:qprofile}
348	For a smooth domain $H$ of order $n$ and $k=\rho n$,
349	\[
350	        \mathcal Q_H(\eta)\ :=\ \max_{\substack{M\mid\gcd(n,k),\ \lceil\eta n\rceil<M\\ k/M\le n/M-1}}\ \log_2\binom{n/M-1}{k/M}
351	\]
352	($-\infty$ over the empty set): the logarithmic size of the largest quotient-core list still active at gap $\eta$.  Correspondingly, every quotient contribution of the form $2^{(\beta(\rho)/\HH(\rho))\mathcal Q_H(\eta)(1+o(1))}$ below is read as zero when the defining maximum is empty.
353	\end{definition}
354	
355	\begin{conjecture}[Quotient-profile corrected list entropy gap]\label{conj:listprofile}
356	Let $C_n=\RS[\F_{q_n},H_n,\rho n]$ with $\mathrm{poly}(n)\le q_n\le2^{o(n)}$ and $q_{D,n}$ the generated-field size, and fix a list target $n^B$.  Necessary for $\Lst(C_n,1-\rho-\eta_n)\le n^B$ is
357	\[
358	        \eta_n\ \ge\ (1-o(1))\,\taustar(\rho,q_{D,n}),
359	        \qquad
360	        \mathcal Q_{H_n}(\eta_n)\ \le\ B\log_2n+O(\log\log n)
361	\]
362	(\cref{cor:genfield-pigeonhole,thm:qcore}); the conjectural positive half is that, after fixed slack in both inequalities, the conditions are sufficient.  On dyadic domains at the deployed rates the scale form is $\eta_n\gtrsim\max\{\taustar(\rho,q_{D,n}),\,\Theta_{\rho,B}(1/\log n)\}$.
363	\end{conjecture}
364	
365	\section{Characteristic-zero inverse quotient theorem}\label{sec:upstairs}
366	
367	We now prove that, over characteristic zero, the quotient-core phenomenon is the entire prefix-fiber structure for $2$-power roots.  This is the toric Manin--Mumford/torsion-coset part of the program (compare the torsion-coset theorem \cite{Laurent84} and the theory of vanishing sums of roots of unity \cite{CJ76,LL00}), but for $2$-primary roots it has an elementary proof.
368	
369	Let $n=2^m$, let $\zeta=e^{2\pi i/n}$, and identify $\mu_n=\langle\zeta\rangle$ with the complex $n$-th roots of unity.  For $h:\mu_n\to\Z$, define
370	\[
371	        \widehat h(j)=\sum_{x\in\mu_n}h(x)x^j\in\Z[\zeta].
372	\]
373	
374	\begin{lemma}[Antipodal identity]\label{lem:antipodal}
375	Let $n'=2^r$ with $r\ge1$, and let $g:\mu_{n'}\to\Z$.  If
376	\[
377	        \sum_{y\in\mu_{n'}}g(y)y=0,
378	\]
379	then $g(y)=g(-y)$ for every $y\in\mu_{n'}$.
380	\end{lemma}
381	
382	\begin{proof}
383	Write $y=\zeta_{n'}^a$.  The sum becomes
384	\[
385	        \sum_{a=0}^{n'/2-1}\big(g(\zeta_{n'}^a)-g(-\zeta_{n'}^a)\big)\zeta_{n'}^a.
386	\]
387	The powers $1,\zeta_{n'},\ldots,\zeta_{n'}^{n'/2-1}$ are linearly independent over $\Q$ because $\Phi_{n'}(X)=X^{n'/2}+1$.  Hence every coefficient difference is zero.
388	\end{proof}
389	
390	\begin{theorem}[Inverse quotient theorem over $\C$]\label{thm:upstairs}
391	Let $n=2^m$, $1\le\sigma<n$, and let $M_0$ be the least power of two with $M_0>\sigma$.  Let $S,T\subseteq\mu_n$ have the same cardinality and satisfy
392	\[
393	        e_j(S)=e_j(T)\qquad(1\le j\le\sigma)
394	\]
395	over $\C$.  Then $S\triangle T$ is a union of cosets of the subgroup $\mu_{M_0}\le\mu_n$.  Equivalently, $\mathbf 1_S-\mathbf 1_T$ is constant on $\mu_{M_0}$-cosets.  Conversely, every such quotient rearrangement preserves all $e_j$ for $1\le j<M_0$.
396	\end{theorem}
397	
398	\begin{proof}
399	By Newton identities in characteristic zero, equality of $e_1,\ldots,e_\sigma$ and equality of cardinalities imply equality of the first $\sigma$ power sums.  Thus, for $h=\mathbf 1_S-\mathbf 1_T$,
400	\[
401	        \widehat h(j)=0\qquad(1\le j\le\sigma).
402	\]
403	It is enough to use the dyadic frequencies.  Let $M_0=2^a$.  Since $2^{a-1}\le\sigma$, we have
404	\[
405	        \widehat h(1)=\widehat h(2)=\cdots=\widehat h(2^{a-1})=0
406	\]
407	at the listed dyadic powers.
408	
409	We prove by induction that vanishing at $1,2,\ldots,2^i$ in this dyadic sense forces invariance under $\mu_{2^{i+1}}$.  For $i=0$, $\widehat h(1)=0$ and \Cref{lem:antipodal} give invariance under $\mu_2$.  Suppose $h$ is invariant under $\mu_{2^i}$ and $\widehat h(2^i)=0$.  The map $x\mapsto x^{2^i}$ sends $\mu_n$ onto $\mu_{n/2^i}$ with fibers the $\mu_{2^i}$-cosets.  Let $h^\ast(y)$ be the common value on the fiber above $y$.  Then
410	\[
411	        0=\widehat h(2^i)=2^i\sum_{y\in\mu_{n/2^i}}h^\ast(y)y.
412	\]
413	By \Cref{lem:antipodal}, $h^\ast(y)=h^\ast(-y)$.  Pulling this identity back says that $h$ is invariant under a primitive $2^{i+1}$-st root of unity, hence under $\mu_{2^{i+1}}$.  Iterating to $i=a-1$ gives $\mu_{M_0}$-invariance.
414	
415	For the converse, if $h$ is constant on $\mu_{M_0}$-cosets, then
416	\[
417	        \widehat h(j)=\sum_{\alpha\mu_{M_0}}h(\alpha)\alpha^j\sum_{\kappa\in\mu_{M_0}}\kappa^j=0
418	\]
419	whenever $M_0\nmid j$, in particular for $1\le j<M_0$.  Newton identities convert the corresponding power-sum preservation into preservation of $e_1,\ldots,e_{M_0-1}$ for equal-size subsets.
420	\end{proof}
421	
422	\begin{corollary}[Polynomial size of characteristic-zero fibers]\label{cor:upstairs-poly}
423	Every characteristic-zero prefix fiber in $\binom{\mu_n}{s}$ has size at most
424	\[
425	        2^{n/M_0}\le 2^{n/\sigma}.
426	\]
427	If $\sigma\ge Cn/\log_2n$, then every such fiber has size at most $n^{1/C}$.  With fixed density one may sharpen this to
428	\[
429	        2^{(n/M_0)\HH(\rho)+o(n/M_0)}\le n^{\HH(\rho)/C+o(1)}.
430	\]

435	\end{proof}
